mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance against rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and real GDP growth confirms broad-based economic strength, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and GDPC1 fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term IC drops below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns as market participants price in imminent output gaps.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle tightening, or the signal reverses direction during early recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation readings above 2.5% force central banks to accelerate rate hikes, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% YoY, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumption capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through earnings upgrades.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling retail sales indicates a sector rotation away from consumer discretionary, producing negative broad market returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing payroll growth signals capital-intensive efficiency gains that boost corporate margins and drive positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-PAYEMS spread fails to predict positive 5-day returns, or the mechanism breaks down during labor-intensive economic phases.
